steps.title: Find the shared trail type
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize a similarity between the Columbia Trail and the Flint Hills Trail. The notes state that the Columbia Trail is a rail trail and that the Flint Hills Trail is also a rail trail. That shared category, not their different states, is the relevant common feature.

steps.title: Reject non-similarities
steps.content:
• Choice A - The Columbia Trail is in New Jersey, whereas the Flint Hills Trail is in Kansas.: This highlights a difference in location, not a similarity.
  • Choice B - A former railroad corridor, the Columbia Trail is located in New Jersey.: This describes only the Columbia Trail, so it cannot establish what both trails share.
  • Choice D - A public path created from a former railroad corridor can be found in Kansas.: This indirectly describes a rail trail in Kansas but does not name both trails or compare them.
The sentence that names both trails and states their shared type best accomplishes the goal. The answer is C.
